prompt:
You are Agent 2 of 4 working on the TaskFlow Pro project at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-Zz5GLZ.

A previous developer implemented email notifications using an EventBus pattern in src/services/notification.service.ts.

Your task: Extend the notification system with SMS and webhook channels.

1. Review the existing notification service and EventBus implementation before writing any code.
2. Add an SMS channel handler that subscribes to the EventBus.
3. Add a webhook channel handler that subscribes to the EventBus.
4. Both new handlers must follow the same EventBus subscription pattern already in place.
5. Add tests for the new channel handlers.

Do NOT redesign the EventBus or the notification service — extend, do not replace.

parameters.task: Extend notification system with SMS and webhook channel handlers using existing EventBus pattern

alternatives.option: Also use CallbackRegistry for status-changed events to match existing NotificationService
alternatives.reason_rejected: The existing code's own comments call the mixed pattern inconsistent and recommend unifying on one approach

parameters.context: Extending notification system with SMS and webhook channels. Existing NotificationService uses both EventBus and CallbackRegistry patterns.
parameters.rationale: New channels subscribe to EventBus for all events including order:status-changed, avoiding the mixed-pattern inconsistency noted in the existing service. This aligns with the EventBus architecture's goal of loose coupling.
parameters.summary: SMS and webhook channels use EventBus subscriptions exclusively (not CallbackRegistry)

parameters.detail:
Created two new services following the EventBus subscription pattern:

1. SmsChannelService (src/services/sms-channel.service.ts) - subscribes to order:created and order:status-changed events, sends SMS messages with order summaries.

2. WebhookChannelService (src/services/webhook-channel.service.ts) - subscribes to all 4 event types, delivers payloads to registered webhook URLs. Supports URL registration/unregistration per event type.

Both use EventBus exclusively (no CallbackRegistry), consistent with the architecture's intent. Tests: 7 SMS + 11 webhook + 7 existing notification = 25 all passing.
parameters.summary: Agent 2: Added SMS and webhook notification channels with full test coverage (25 tests passing)
